expressions.notes:
깊다 = to be deep
깊은 is the adjective form of 깊다.

벌써 = already
잠들다 = to fall asleep
-(으)면 안 되다 = shouldn't; must not

expressions.english: It's too hot, so I can't sleep.
expressions.notes:
덥다 = to be hot
-어/-여 = because, since
잠이 오다 = to be sleepy; to feel drowsy
잠이 인 오다 = to be unable to sleep

expressions.english: This morning, I got up late.
He talks a lot in his sleep.
expressions.notes:
그 사람 = that person; he, she
잠꼬대 = talking in one's sleep
심하다 = to be excessive, too much; to be harsh

expressions.english: I sleep too much and it's a problem.
expressions.notes:
저 = (polite) I; me
문제 = problem, issue; thing; matter

expressions.english: What do you do when you feel sleepy while you are driving?
expressions.notes:
운전하다 = to drive
-다가 = as a result of V-ing
-(으)면 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V
어떻게 = how
-(으)면 어떻게 해요? = What do you do when + S + V ?; What am I supposed to do if you + V?

expressions.english: I went to bed later than usual yesterday.
expressions.notes:
평소 = usual day, ordinary day
-보다 = than + N

expressions.korean: 어제 잠을 설쳐서 오늘 너무 피곤해요.
expressions.english: I didn't sleep well last night, so I feel very tired today.
expressions.notes: 잠을 설치다 = to not sleep very well

expressions.english: I fell asleep on the bus and missed my stop.
expressions.notes:
버스 = bus
내리다 = to get off (from a vehicle)
곳 = place
놓치다 = to miss

expressions.korean: 이렇게 시끄러운 데에서 어떻게 잠을 자요?
expressions.english: How do you sleep in such a noisy place?
expressions.notes:
이렇게 = like this, in this manner; so +adjective
시끄럽다 = to be noisy, loud
시끄러운 is the adjective form of 시끄럽다.
-ㄴ, -데 = (adjective) + place
